findings.title: Hardcoded high-entropy string (potential secret/credential)
findings.description: A string literal with Shannon entropy 4.58 bits was detected. High-entropy strings embedded in source code are often credentials, API keys, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Move secrets to environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., os.environ['API_KEY'], HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).

rule.title: Hardcoded high-entropy string (potential secret/credential)
rule.summary: Flags secrets, credentials, and signing keys embedded directly in source code.
rule.remediation: Load secrets from environment variables or a secrets manager and rotate compromised values.

findings.title: CWE-502: Unsafe deserialization at line 38
findings.description: Unsafe pickle deserialization at L38: `return pickle.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))`. If the data comes from an untrusted source, an attacker can achieve RCE.
findings.suggestion: Use JSON. If pickle is required, verify data integrity with HMAC before loading.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: return pickle.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))
AFTER:                      return json.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))

rule.title: Python unsafe deserialization pattern
rule.summary: Flags direct use of unsafe deserializers such as `pickle.loads`, `marshal.loads`, or unsafe `yaml.load`.
rule.remediation: Use JSON or verify integrity and safety before deserializing.

findings.title: CWE-502: Unsafe deserialization at line 71
findings.description: Unsafe pickle deserialization at L71: `previous_value = pickle.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))`. If the data comes from an untrusted source, an attacker can achieve RCE.
findings.suggestion: Use JSON. If pickle is required, verify data integrity with HMAC before loading.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: previous_value = pickle.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))
AFTER:                          previous_value = json.loads(zlib.decompress(f.read()))

rule.title: Python unsafe deserialization pattern
rule.summary: Flags direct use of unsafe deserializers such as `pickle.loads`, `marshal.loads`, or unsafe `yaml.load`.
rule.remediation: Use JSON or verify integrity and safety before deserializing.

findings.title: CWE-502: Unsafe deserialization at line 152
findings.description: Unsafe pickle deserialization at L152: `exp = pickle.load(f)`. If the data comes from an untrusted source, an attacker can achieve RCE.
findings.suggestion: Use JSON. If pickle is required, verify data integrity with HMAC before loading.
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: exp = pickle.load(f)
AFTER:              exp = json.load(f)

rule.title: Python unsafe deserialization pattern
rule.summary: Flags direct use of unsafe deserializers such as `pickle.loads`, `marshal.loads`, or unsafe `yaml.load`.
rule.remediation: Use JSON or verify integrity and safety before deserializing.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in _list_cache_files() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `_list_cache_files()` passes `fname` (possibly user-controlled or DB-sourced) to `os.path.join()` at L167 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: Implicit None return in _cull() — can fall off end
findings.description: `_cull()` has 1 branches that return a value and 1 that return None implicitly. Callers that do not check for None will encounter AttributeError or incorrect logic.
findings.suggestion: Ensure all code paths return an explicit value, or annotate the return type.

rule.title: Inconsistent return paths causing implicit None
rule.summary: Flags functions whose branches mix explicit return values with implicit `None` fall-through.
rule.remediation: Ensure all paths return an explicit value or annotate the optional return contract clearly.

findings.title: CWE-22: Path traversal in open_resource() via os.path.join()
findings.description: `open_resource()` passes `resource` (possibly user-controlled or DB-sourced) to `os.path.join()` at L123 without sanitization. `../` sequences can escape the intended directory.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize: `from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename; safe = secure_filename(filename)`. Verify resolved path: `assert os.path.realpath(p).startswith(BASE_DIR)`.

rule.title: Python path join with unsanitized user input
rule.summary: Flags `os.path.join` or similar path composition with tainted user input.
rule.remediation: Normalize against a safe root and reject any resolved path that escapes it.

findings.title: CWE-307: No rate limiting on authentication route at line 39
findings.description: Authentication route at L39 has no rate-limiting decorator or middleware in scope: `@app.post("/login")`. An attacker can brute-force credentials, OTPs, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Add rate limiting: `flask-limiter` for Flask (`@limiter.limit('5/minute')`), `slowapi` for FastAPI, or a middleware-level rate limiter.

rule.title: Python auth/sensitive route missing rate limiting
rule.summary: Flags Flask/FastAPI/Django authentication or sensitive routes (login, MFA, password-reset, token-refresh) where no rate-limiting library is detected in file scope, enabling brute-force attacks.
rule.remediation: Use `flask-limiter` (`@limiter.limit('5/minute')`), `slowapi` for FastAPI, `django-ratelimit`, or a reverse-proxy rate limiter in front of sensitive endpoints.

findings.title: CWE-307: No rate limiting on authentication route at line 193
findings.description: Authentication route at L193 has no rate-limiting decorator or middleware in scope: `@app.post("/login")`. An attacker can brute-force credentials, OTPs, or tokens.
findings.suggestion: Add rate limiting: `flask-limiter` for Flask (`@limiter.limit('5/minute')`), `slowapi` for FastAPI, or a middleware-level rate limiter.

rule.title: Python auth/sensitive route missing rate limiting
rule.summary: Flags Flask/FastAPI/Django authentication or sensitive routes (login, MFA, password-reset, token-refresh) where no rate-limiting library is detected in file scope, enabling brute-force attacks.
rule.remediation: Use `flask-limiter` (`@limiter.limit('5/minute')`), `slowapi` for FastAPI, `django-ratelimit`, or a reverse-proxy rate limiter in front of sensitive endpoints.

findings.title: Excessive complexity in _post_process() (CC=17)
findings.description: `_post_process()` at L380 has cyclomatic complexity 17. Functions above 15 are hard to test, debug, and maintain.
findings.suggestion: Extract sub-functions for distinct logic branches or use lookup tables.

rule.title: Cyclomatic complexity hotspot
rule.summary: Flags overly branchy functions that are statistically harder to review, test, and secure.
rule.remediation: Split complex control flow into smaller helpers with single responsibilities.

findings.title: CWE-327: Weak password hashing (MD5) at line 669
findings.description: `MD5` is cryptographically broken for password storage at L669. No salt is used; rainbow tables and GPU brute-force make this trivial to crack.
findings.suggestion: Use bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt: `bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt())`
findings.auto_fix:
BEFORE: hash = hashlib.md5((salt + password).encode()).hexdigest()
AFTER:          import bcrypt
                hashed = bcrypt.hashpw((salt + password, bcrypt.gensalt())

rule.title: Python weak password hashing
rule.summary: Flags MD5/SHA1/SHA224 used for password hashing or similar credential protection.
rule.remediation: Use bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt for passwords instead of fast general-purpose hashes.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via eval() at line 32
findings.description: `eval()` is called with dynamic content at L32: `eval(req.body.preTax)`. If the argument is attacker-influenced, arbitrary JavaScript can execute.
findings.suggestion: Remove `eval()`. Parse data with `JSON.parse()` or use a safe expression interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via eval()
rule.summary: `eval()` called.
rule.remediation: Eliminate eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression library.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via eval() at line 33
findings.description: `eval()` is called with dynamic content at L33: `eval(req.body.afterTax)`. If the argument is attacker-influenced, arbitrary JavaScript can execute.
findings.suggestion: Remove `eval()`. Parse data with `JSON.parse()` or use a safe expression interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via eval()
rule.summary: `eval()` called.
rule.remediation: Eliminate eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression library.

findings.title: CWE-95: Code injection via eval() at line 34
findings.description: `eval()` is called with dynamic content at L34: `eval(req.body.roth)`. If the argument is attacker-influenced, arbitrary JavaScript can execute.
findings.suggestion: Remove `eval()`. Parse data with `JSON.parse()` or use a safe expression interpreter.

rule.title: Code injection via eval()
rule.summary: `eval()` called.
rule.remediation: Eliminate eval(). Use JSON.parse() for data, or a safe expression library.
